PART TWO
MINUTES, TWENTY-SIXTH GENERAL ASSEMBLY
First Session - Tuesday Afternoon
June 30, 1998
26-1 Assembly Called to Order and Opening Worship
The Twenty-Sixth General Assembly of the Presbyterian Church in America
gathered for the opening worship service at 1:30 PM on Tuesday, June 30, 1998 in the
St. Louis Ballroom of the Adam’s Mark Hotel in St. Louis, Missouri.
Prelude Grand Processional on “Praise to the Lord, the Almighty ’’ Shaw
When I Survey the Wondrous Cross Martin
God calls us to worship TE W. Wilson Benton
we respond in adoration o Come, Let Us Sing to the Lord #13
We invoke God’s presence TE Rodney Stortz
We respond and pray Non Nobis Domine Patrick Doyle
Missouri Presbytery Joint Choir
Kathy Chapell - Director
God calls us to confession RE Mark Belz
We confess our sins Daniel 9:4-17 RE Mark Belz
god assures us of pardoning grace out of the depths Gerg Wilbur
Missouri Presbytery Joint Choir
Betsy Tyvoll - Director
We respond with gifts Alleluia Tulerunt Palestrina
Covenant College Brass
Timothy Steele - Director
We praise God for His blessings Doxology #731
God calls us to His Word TE James Drexler
We prepare to hear His Word Guide Me, O Thou Great Jehovah #598
Missouri Presbytery Joint Choir
Covenant College Brass
God exhorts us in His Word “Faith and Obedience, Part 2: A Ruling Elder’s Story”
RE Samuel Duncan
We respond to God’s Word and prepare for His table
lamb of god RE Jerome Merritt - Tenor
Verse 1 Your only Son, no sin to hide, but You have sent Him from Your side,
To walk upon this guilty sod, and to become the Lamb of God.
Chorus: O Lamb of God, sweet Lamb of God, I love the holy Lamb of God!
O wash me in His precious blood my Jesus Christ, the Lamb of God.

<a id="ga26-p22"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=26 pdf_page=24 printed_page=22 -->

Verse 2: Your gift of love, they crucified, they laughed and scorned Him as He died,
the humble King they named a fraud and sacrificed the Lamb of God Chorus.
Verse 3: 1 was so lost, 1 should have died, but You have brought me to our side,
to be led by Your staff and rod, and to be called a lamb of God Chorus.
We confess our faith Apostles’ Creed TE Doug Madi
God invites us to His table TE Jeff Meyers
We receive the Lord’s Supper TE Jeff Meyers
God blesses TE Barry Henning
We respond to God’s grace Jesus, Thy Blood and Righteousness #520
Postlude Carillon de Westminster Vierne
Following worship, the Assembly recessed for 30 minutes at 3:00 PM

26-2 Reconvening for Business
Moderator RE Samuel Duncan reconvened the Assembly for business at 3:25 PM
with prayer by TEs, Eugene Case and Andy Silman, REs William Moore and Frank
Young
26-3 Declaration of Quorum and Enrollment
The Moderator declared a quorum present with 884 Teaching Elders, 397 Ruling
Elders, and 12 Ruling Elder alternates enrolled

26-4 Adoption of the Docket
The ninth draft of the docket was declared adopted with the following
amendments: To set the Report of the Standing Judicial Commission on Wednesday at
3:00 p mas a special order, To set a partial report of the Committee of Commissioners
on Bills and Overtures for Tuesday morning at 10:00 a mas a special order, and to set a
partial report of the Committee of Commissioners on Bills and Overtures concerning
Overtures 5 and 18 and Communication 4 to proceed the report of the standing

<a id="ga26-p47"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=26 pdf_page=49 printed_page=47 -->

Judicial Commission. A request was also received to move the Special Report from
Theological Seminaries Involved in Starting the PC A to an earlier time in the docket.
26-5 Election of Moderator
The Moderator opened the floor for nominations for Moderator of the 26th
General Assembly. TE Paul Settle nominated TE Kennedy Smartt, North Georgia
Presbytery, who was elected by acclamation. RE Samuel Duncan offered prayer for the
new Moderator. The Moderator assumed the chair, and shared with the Assembly a
report concerning the health of TE Donald Patterson The Assembly elected TE
Patterson Honorary Moderator of the 26th General Assembly, and prayer was offered for
the Pattersons by TE Paul Settle. It was ordered that this action be communicated to TE
Patterson as soon as practical.
The Chairman of the Administrative Committee, TE William Fox, presented to
the retiring moderator a plaque in token of the Assembly’s appreciation for his year of
service as Moderator and offered a prayer of thanks.
26-6 Greetings from Dignitaries
TE Jim Drexler, Chairman of the Local Arrangement Committee, introduced
United States Congressman James Talent, a member of Twin Oaks Presbyterian Church
in St. Louis. Congressman Talent brought greetings and shared his personal faith in
Christ and gratitude for the PCA.
26-7 Election of Recording and Assistant Clerks
On nomination by the Stated Clerk, TEs David Dively, J. Robert Fiol, and Steven
Meyerhoff were elected recording clerks; TE Robert Ferguson and RE John B White,
Jr. were appointed assistant parliamentarians, RE Walter Lastovica was appointed
timekeeper, RE Ed Nalley sound manager, and TE James A. smith chairman of the floor
clerks.
26-8 Personal Resolution #1 From TE Barney Heyward
By common consent, the following was received, ordered spread upon the
Minutes, sent to Honorary Moderator Donald Patterson, and made available for others
to add their signatures:
since the days of Joshua, the people of god have made memorials to
preserve a record of god acting in mighty ways to deliver his people. God’s
gracious acts bum themselves into the mind of the generation that sees them, but
with succeeding generations, that memory can fade. Memorials, then, are a
testimony to God’s greatness and humanity’s frailty; a means to remind
succeeding generations that our God is a God who works mightily to redeem His
people.
it is in this spirit, in the tradition of these memorials, that we, the
undersigned, representing anew generation of leaders within the Presbyterian Church in America, would offer this letter of thanks. We wish to express our

<a id="ga26-p48"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=26 pdf_page=50 printed_page=48 -->

deep appreciation to those fathers of our denomination who, through great personal sacrifice, through firm conviction of the preciousness of the gospel, and with a reluctance that betokened a deep love for their brothers, led congregations out of the Presbyterian Church of the United States, to form the Presbyterian Church in America.

We are grateful for their example. We are grateful for the denomination that has come into being as a result of their labors. Most of all, we are grateful that this has not been a work of man, but a work of God, who has deigned to use the efforts of fallen human beings to further His glory.

26-9 Special Order: Celebration of God’s Mighty Acts through Mission to North America

TE Cortez Cooper, Coordinator of MNA Committee, led the Assembly in prayer and shared remarks on the work of the Committee under the leadership of former coordinators TEs Larry Mills, Phillip Clark, Terry Gyger and himself. He reported that 40% of all PCA churches (505) have been planted by the PCA since her inception. TEs

<a id="ga26-p49"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=26 pdf_page=51 printed_page=49 -->

Phillip Clark, Tim Keller, and Ken Cross shared experiences, encouragement and
exhortation Video vignettes were shown which highlighted RUM and ethnic outreach
(which will be the focus of the 1998 WIC Love Gift).
26-10 Special Order: 25th Anniversary Celebration “Origins of the PCA”
TE paul settle led the assembly in prayer and presented a summary of the origins
and history of the PCA.
26-11 Recess
The Assembly recessed at 5:40 pm with prayer byte Settle.
Second Session - Wednesday Morning
July 1, 1998
26-12 Assembly Reconvenes
The Assembly reconvened at 8:00 AM on July 1, 1998 with worship led by
Korean Southeastern Presbytery. Following worship, the Assembly was led in prayer by
TE John Batusic.

COMMUNICATION 2 From the Christian Reformed Church in North America (IRC)

COMMUNICATION 3 From Presbyterian Evangelistic Fellowship
Dear Dr Gilchrist:
I am thankful for your work and dedication on the behalf of the PCA. I sent out
the following letter to all the Stated Clerks of the Presbyteries. I asked them to include
this letter in the docket for their upcoming meetings and if they could read this letter at
the next Presbytery meeting
I want to wish the Presbyterian Church in America happy 25th anniversary in
1998 We want to thank God for His blessing on the PCA and the pastors, elders, and
churches in your Presbytery. Let us rejoice and give God the glory for all the boards and
agencies of the PCA. We celebrate Christ the King, who is the head of the PCA As
Christ said in Matthew 16:18: “ .. I will build my church; and the gates of hell shall not
prevail against it ”
Our organization, Presbyterian Evangelistic Fellowship, will be celebrating its
40th anniversary in 1998 The founder of PEF, William E. Hill, PEF’s second executive
director, Ben Wilkinson, and its preaching evangelists found a hunger among
Presbyterians for a denomination that would uphold Scripture as the inspired Word of
God, to the Reformed faith, and to the Great Commission PEF played a vital role in the
formation of the PCA. Bill Hill, James Baird, and Ben Wilkinson, representing PEF
along with representatives of three additional bodies became a group of twelve within
the PCUS to form what was to be known as the Steering Committee for a Continuing
Church. With our commitment to the Great Commission, PEF sensed the need for
overseas evangelism and formed the Executive Committee for Overseas Evangelism
(ECOE). ECOE’s twenty one missionaries, candidates and staff, became the initial
nucleus for what is now Mission to the World. With these missionaries came a $100,000
financial gift from PEF to establish this newly formed mission agency.

<a id="ga26-p53"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=26 pdf_page=55 printed_page=53 -->

Not only in the foreign field, but also for domestic missions, PEF provided a
framework in which the new denomination could begin its program. A structure known
as Mission to the United States was established. When it was turned over intact to the
new denomination, it had helped establish 35 congregations in the PC A The transfer
included 12 organizing pastors and $40,000.
After 40 years, PEF is still training, assisting, and practicing Biblical evangelism
anywhere, anytime, with anyone. Presently, we are doing Biblical evangelism in the US
and 20 countries. We cooperate with agencies of Presbyterian and Reformed Churches
that promote Biblical evangelism. We would be glad to come to your churches to help
train your congregation in evangelism. PEF can provide resources like pamphlets and
tracts on evangelism Please pray with us as we are asking God to raise up 35 new PEF
missionaries in 1998. Also, pray for us as we go into the 21st century for the glory of
God and the salvation of souls.
if we can be of assistance to you and your church in preaching, evangelistic and
revival services, mission’s conferences or training in evangelism, please contact PEF
Once again, happy 25th anniversary Presbyterian Church in America .
In Christ,
/s/ Dr. A1 Herrington, Executive Director
Acts 1:8

26-17 Voting on Book of Church Order Amendments
the assembly moved to consideration of amendments to the Book of Church Order proposed by the Twenty-Fifth General Assembly.

<a id="ga26-p56"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=26 pdf_page=58 printed_page=56 -->

ITEM 1. That BCO 13-6 be amended as follows:
[see minutes of 25th GA, 25-45, III, 1, page 178]
13-6. Ministers seeking admission to a Presbytery from other
Presbyteries in the Presbyterian Church in America shall be examined
on Christian experience, and also touching their views in theology, the
Sacraments, and church government. If applicants come from other
denominations, the Presbytery shall examine them thoroughly in
knowledge and views as required by BCO 21-4 and also require them
to answer in the affirmative the questions put to candidates at their
ordination. Ordained ministers from other denominations being
considered by Presbyteries for reception may come under the
extraordinary provisions set forth in BCO 21-4.
Grounds:
As it reads it allows for men to be ordained in the PCA without giving
satisfaction to the presbytery of their knowledge in any area. We are permitted
to examine a man only with reference to his views whether he comes from
another PCA Presbytery, a sister Reformed denomination, or an independent,
Charismatic, Baptist, or some other denomination. This seems to be a weakness
in our polity. We have sought to maintain the highest standards for a PCA
teaching elder. It is our contention that the BCO should require that if a man is
coming to us from another denomination he must sustain the same exam for
ordination, in knowledge and views, as any candidate.
Adopted
FOR 43______ ag AINST______ 1

ITEM 2. That BCO 38-3 be amended by substituting the following:
[seeMinutes of 25th GA, 25-22,First Supplement, II, pp. 132f]
38-3 a. When a member or minister of the Presbyterian Church in America shall attempt to withdraw from the communion of this branch of the
visible Church by affiliating with some other branch (BCO 2-2), if at the time of
the attempt to withdraw he is in good standing, the irregularity shall be recorded,
his new membership acknowledged, and his name removed from the roll But if
at the time of the attempt to withdraw there is a record of an investigation in
process (BCO 31-2), or there are charges (BCO 32-3) concerning the member or
minister, the court of original jurisdiction may retain his name on the rolland
conduct the case, communicating the outcome upon completion of the
proceedings to that member or minister. If the court does not conduct the case,
his new membership shall be acknowledged, his name removed from the roll, and,

<a id="ga26-p58"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=26 pdf_page=60 printed_page=58 -->

at the request of the receiving branch, the matters under investigation or the
charges shall be communicated to them In either case, if the member or minister
is an officer, such removal from the roll shall thereby withdraw from him all
authority to exercise his office as derived from this Church.
b when a member or minister of the Presbyterian Church in America shall attempt to withdraw from the communion of this branch of the
visible church by affiliating with a body judged by the court of original
jurisdiction as failing to maintain the Word and Sacraments in their fundamental
integrity (BCO 2-2), that member or minister shall be warned of his danger, and if
he persists, his name shall be erased from the roll, thereby, so far as the
Presbyterian Church in America is concerned, removing him from membership in
good standing in any branch of the visible Church, he is deemed no longer to be
a member in any body which rightly maintains the Word and Sacraments
in their fundamental integrity, and if an officer, thereby withdrawing from him
all authority to exercise his office as derived from this Church When so acting
the courts hall make full record of the matter and shall notify the offender of its
action.
Adopted

279)

3. That the General Assembly elect Dr. L. Roy Taylor as the third
Stated Clerk of the General Assembly, [see 26-53, p. 189] Adopted
4. That in accordance with RAO 3-4 nominations for Stated Clerk be
permitted from the floor and that a deadline for such nominations be set
at 9:30 a m. on Wednesday, July 1, 1998, in order to give time for the
Theological Examining Committee to interview candidates. Adopted
5 That following a systematic and thorough review by the AC of the Stated
Clerk, Dr Paul R Gilchrist, and his performance with regard to his
responsibilities and duties and his service to the Presbyterian Church in America, the General Assembly commend Dr Paul R Gilchrist for his
outstanding and excellent performance and service to the Church and for
his upholding the dignity and integrity of his office. Further, that the
General Assembly express its deepest appreciation to Dr Gilchrist and
God’s blessing on the eve of his retirement. Adopted
6. That the following overtures be answered in the affirmative: Overtures 9
from Potomac Presbytery, Overture 13 from Heartland Presbytery, and

##### Overture 19 from New Jersey Presbytery, and Overture 29 from Grace

Presbytery “Commendation and Thanks for Dr. Paul Gilchrist’s
Exemplary Service as Stated Clerk.” Adopted

check were presented by TE William Fox on behalf of the AC and General Assembly. Moderator TE Kennedy Smartt led in prayer for the Stated Clerk

##### OVERTURE 9 From Potomac Presbytery

“Commendation and Thanks for Dr Paul Gilchrist’s Exemplary Service
as Stated Clerk”

Whereas, Teaching Elder, Dr Paul Rowland Gilchrist has served the
Presbyterian Church in America as its Stated Clerk since June 1988, and

Whereas, the Presbyterian Church in America has benefited greatly from his
service in this Office and,

Whereas, since his ordination as a teaching elder in October 1956, he has
selflessly served the Church of Jesus Christ with distinction, dedication,
and devotion to the glory of God, often well beyond the extent to which
he has been called, and

Whereas, Dr. Gilchrist is retiring from the Office of the Stated Clerk in June
1998,

Be It Resolved that the General Assembly of the Presbyterian Church in America
in Saint Louis, Missouri, June 30, 1998 commends and extends its
heartfelt thanks to Dr Paul Rowland Gilchrist for his exemplary work and
witness in the Kingdom of the Lord Jesus Christ.
Adopted at the 33rd Stated Meeting of Potomac Presbytery on February 14,
1998. Attested by: Richard R. Larson, Stated Clerk

##### OVERTURE 13 From Heartland Presbytery

“Commendation for Dr. Paul Gilchrist’s Service as Stated Clerk”

Whereas, Dr Paul Gilchrist has served the Presbyterian Church in America for
the past ten years as Stated Clerk, and

<a id="ga26-p62"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=26 pdf_page=64 printed_page=62 -->

Whereas, the 26th General Assembly will be pausing to praise God for His
faithfulness during the 25 years since the denomination was formed, and

Whereas, much of this growth is attributable to the leadership of our Stated
Clerk, and

Whereas, he has announced his desire to retire from this position at the close of
the 26th General Assembly,

Therefore be it resolved that the 26th General Assembly of the Presbyterian Church in America give praise to God for Dr. Gilchrist’s service as Stated
Clerk, and

Be it Further resolved that the General Assembly ask Dr Gilchrist and his wife,
Barbara, and any family members who may be present, to move to the
platform as the Assembly gives praise to God for their service to the King
of the Church.
Adopted at the Spring Stated Meeting of Heartland Presbytery , on March 21,
1998 Attested by: /s/ Lawrence N. Lunceford, Stated Clerk

##### Overture 19 From New Jersey Presbytery, Overture 27 From Southeast

Louisiana Presbytery, and Overture 29 From Grace Presbytery
“Commendation and Thanks for Dr. Paul Gilchrist’s Exemplary Service
as Stated Clerk”
[NOTE: These three overtures have the same text as Overture 9 From
Potomac Presbytery ]

9. The Committee refers the following to the General Assembly Committee
of commissioners for the pc .A Foundation:
(See 26-65, III, 4 and 6, p. 220 For final disposition)

<a id="ga26-p63"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=26 pdf_page=65 printed_page=63 -->

That GA amend the Corporate Bylaws of the PCA, Article VI, Section 5,
the first two paragraphs as follows:
“The Board of Directors of Presbyterian Church in America
Foundation, Inc., shall consist of ten (10) members: (a) Five (5) Six (6)
members in classes elected through the standard nomination and election
procedures (the “at large members”); and (b) one (1) member from each
of the following permanent committees or board of trustees of the
agencies: (1) Christian Education and Publications, {2-) Covenant
College, (3- 2) Covenant Theological Seminary, (4 3) Mission to the
World, and (# 4) Mission to North America (the “Appointed Members”).
the chairman of the board of directors of Presbyterian Church in America Foundation, Inc. shall bean at large member.
The at-large members shall serve for four (4) year terms, and shall
be divided into four (4) classes, each class being as nearly equal in number
as possible, so that the terms of approximately one-fourth of the at-large
members shall expire each year. Directors shall be eligible for reelection
to a secondfour-year term after which there must be a one-year interval
before further election.
10. That the 26th GA accept the invitation from Evangel Presbytery to host
the 30th General Assembly in June 2002 in the Birmingham area subject to
available suitable facilities. Adopted
11. That the 26th GA respond to Overture 8 from Heritage Presbytery
“Cease Publishing Private Information in Yearbook’’ by referring it to the
AC with instructions to the Stated Clerk to review with legal counsel and
other knowledgeable persons the questions of privacy raised, and to
report to the October 1998 meeting of AC in order to implement any
appropriate recommendations both for the AC and for other committees
and agencies. Adopted

##### OVERTURE 8 From Heritage Presbytery

“Cease Publishing Private Information in Yearbook”

Whereas, the PCA Yearbook annually contains enough personal information
about PCA ministers to potentially severely compromise privacy of its
ministers to persons of ill will,

Whereas, in years past such information was gathered prior to modem privacy
problems.

Whereas, material gathered from the PCA Yearbook together with other
reasonably available information could significantly compromise the
financial integrity of ministers by persons o fill will,

Whereas, this publishing this information provides no significant useful
legitimate purpose,

<a id="ga26-p64"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=26 pdf_page=66 printed_page=64 -->

Therefore be it resolved, that the General Assembly instruct the Stated Clerk’s
Office to cease gathering or publishing mother’s maiden name and
location of birth of ministers,
And to review other data gathered for the Yearbook and cease publishing
information about churches, agencies or ministers that is of little use and
may be potentially damaging.
And that the General Assembly instruct the other committees and agencies to
review their gathering of and distributing of personal information No
personal information should be gathered unless there is significant
legitimate purpose. If so gathered, it must be kept confidential.
Attested by: /s/ Bruce B Howes, Stated Clerk
12. That Overture 6 from Eastern Carolina Presbytery “Permit Vendors to
Sell at GA Booths” be answered in the negative Adopted
Grounds:
1. Monitoring local government requirements for sales taxes and
business licenses for exhibitors would be cumbersome for the AC
staff
2. Monitoring the sales items of the exhibitors to ensure that the
items offered for sale meet appropriate doctrinal and other
standards, and that vendors are not selling the same items as the
CE&P Bookstore would be an additional burden for the AC staff.

##### OVERTURE 6 From Eastern Carolina Presbytery

“Permit vendors to sell at ga Booths”

Whereas, the General Assembly, in addition to its work as a court, also provides
a forum for the encouragement of the commissioners in attendance;

Whereas, the General Assembly from the beginning has permitted vendors to set
up booths to display their materials,

Whereas, this practice provides a valuable service in giving commissioners an
opportunity to see materials and talk to company representatives which
might otherwise be difficult to do;

Whereas, these vendors pay a significant fee to have such booths,

Whereas, only the PCA Bookstore is allowed to sell materials at the Assembly
and other vendors are not permitted to sell their goods and services;

Whereas, the PCA bookstore does not carry many of the goods which vendors
would be willing to make available;

Whereas, there have been vendors who no longer display their goods and
services because this policy greatly hinders their recouping their costs in
having such a booth,

Whereas, the present policy works to the disadvantage and inconvenience of the
commissioners at the General Assembly;

<a id="ga26-p65"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=26 pdf_page=67 printed_page=65 -->

It is moved that the General Assembly direct the Permanent Committee on
Administration to allow vendors to sell those goods and services at the
General Assembly which are either not available through the PCA
Bookstore booth or having been available at the booth have sold out.
Adopted at the Winter Stated Meeting of Eastern Carolina Presbytery,
January 24, 1998. Attested by: /s/ Austin Leake, Stated Clerk

26. That the 26th General Assembly set the commissioner registration fee for
1999 at $ 140. Adopted
Grounds:
The average cost of General Assembly, per commissioner, for the last
three years is $134.
Costs are higher when held in convention centers and hotels than they are
when held in our churches, due primarily to the rental of the meeting
spaces and other services. Our scheduled meetings, through 2001, are all
in hotels and convention centers.
The 23rd General Assembly approved including funding of a portion of the
unfunded mandates from the General Assembly in the registration fee.
the chart reporting the costs of the un funded mandates shows that the
prorated costs of the Standing Judicial Commission alone were $35 per
commissioner in 1997.

28. That the 26th General Assembly not take any action on the CEO
Compensation Chart since there was no up to date comprehensive study

and report to the October 1998 meeting of the AC so that it may be used

current PCA guidelines as stated in the CEO Compensation - Guidelines

Adopted
29. That the 26th General Assembly adopt the following resolution
concerning Donald B. Patterson: Adopted

Whereas, Donald B. Patterson was elected by the Presbyterian
Churchmen United to serve on the Steering Committee for a
Continuing Presbyterian Church, and was elected by that
Committee to serve as its chairman, and

Whereas, he served faithfully and efficiently in guiding the work of that
committee which led to the formation of the Presbyterian Church in America, and

Whereas, he served as the first chairman of the permanent committee on
Mission to the World and had a very significant part in the
formation of its initial policies which have led MTW into great
prominence and fruitfulness in world evangelism, and

Whereas, he resigned as Pastor of the First Presbyterian Church in
Jackson, MS, in order to devote himself as Pastor at Large for
Mission to the World, visiting and encouraging missionaries and
their families in all areas of the world, and

Whereas, he has given himself to speaking in the churches at home,
encouraging their spiritual growth and awareness to world
evangelization, and

Whereas, he has recently gone through chemotherapy for cancer which
resulted in severe damage to his heart that has prevented his
attendance at this Assembly, and

Whereas, it was the intent of the Twenty-Fifth Anniversary Committee to
nominate him to serve this Twenty-Sixth Assembly as its
Moderator,

<a id="ga26-p68"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=26 pdf_page=70 printed_page=68 -->

Therefore Be it Resolved that this Twenty-Sixth General Assembly
express to Dr. Patterson its heartfelt thanks for the tremendous
contribution he has made to the formation and development of the
Presbyterian Church in America, that it elect him as an honorary
Moderator of this Assembly, and that it pause to pray for the
restoration to robust health and continuing ministry.
NOTE: TE William F. Joseph led in prayer for TE Donald Patterson
30. That the 26th General Assembly adopt the following resolution
concerning the Presbyterian Evangelistic Fellowship: Adopted

Whereas, the Presbyterian Evangelistic Fellowship was founded
in 1958 to give expression to the commitment of Presbyterians to
evangelism, revival and the fulfillment of the great Commission,
and

Whereas, the PEF began when the Rev. William E. Hill, Jr.,
resigned his pastorate at the West End Presbyterian Church in
Hopewell, VA to devote himself to a ministry of evangelism and
renewal across the Presbyterian Church, US, and

Whereas, God blessed that ministry to the end that by 1970 there
were twelve full time Presbyterian evangelists serving with PEF,
and

Whereas, PEF saw the need for evangelistic Presbyterian
missionaries to serve overseas and formed the Executive
Committee for Overseas Evangelism to recruit the missionaries
and raise their support, and

Whereas. pe f was one of the four organizations which
participated in the formation of the Steering Committee for a
Continuing Presbyterian Church that would be true to the
Scriptures and obedient to the Great Commission, electing TEs
William E. Hill, H. Bennett Wilkinson, and James M Baird to
serve on the Committee, and

Whereas, when the PCA held its First General Assembly in
Birmingham in 1973,PEF’s Executive Committee for Overseas
Evangelism presented the new denomination with its missionaries,
staff and funds to help get MTW started, and

Whereas, the First General Assembly recommended that PEF
continue as an independent agency serving alongside the PCA,
which is has loyally and faithfully done, and has ministered in
evangelism, church renewal, overseas missions, church planting,
and inner city work in the areas where the PCA has not
concentrated its energies or resources, working today in seventeen
states, twenty foreign countries, through almost 200 ministers,
Christian workers and their spouses, and

<a id="ga26-p69"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=26 pdf_page=71 printed_page=69 -->

Whereas, PEF has made a tremendous contribution to the
Kingdom, the Church and in particular the PCA through her
devoted and continuing ministry, and countries under the
leadership of Rev. J. Allen Herrington, and

Whereas, PEF is this year celebrating her 40th Anniversary,

Therefore be it Resolved, that the 26th General Assembly rise in
recognition of this special occasion and express gratitude to God
for the men of vision who have served the PEF and the PCA in
lifting high the cross of Christ, and to pray for God’s continued
blessing upon this very notable and significant ministry.
NOTE: TE A1 Herrington led in prayer for PEF.
NOTE: The Committee reported that a quorum was not present when they dealt
with recommendations 31 and 32, however, the Assembly agreed to
consider them.
31 That the 26th General Assembly answer the following Personal
Resolution #3 from TE Thomas Wenger in the affirmative as amended:
Adopted as amended

Whereas, Scripture in I Cor. 6:1-8 frowns on Christian brothers contending
against one another in the secular courts,

Whereas, the board of directors may have to pursue redress through the civil
courts against a brother over avery trivial matter,

Therefore, be it resolved that the 26th General Assembly forbid the taking of
civil or legal action by the Board of Directors or any other agency and/or
employee of the Presbyterian Church in America, against any member or
congregation of the PCA, relative to the use of the PCA trademark until
every ecclesiastical means has been exhausted.
32. That the 26th General Assembly answer the following Personal
Resolution #2 from TE David Longacre in the negative. Adopted

Whereas, the viewing of pornography is a violation of the seventh
commandment (Exodus 20:14), as clarified by our Lord Jesus Christ in
Matthew 5:27-28,

Whereas, scripture reveals and experience confirms that the viewing of
pornography leads to deeper sexual sins, and slavery to sin (Psalm 101:3,
NASB),

Whereas, the 13th General Assembly of the Presbyterian Church in America, has
made a plain statement in opposition to pornography, and, ‘Urges the
members of the churches of our denomination to be involved in various
efforts of fighting pornography in our land,” (13th GA, 1985, 13-65, III,
5, p. 133);

<a id="ga26-p70"></a>
<!-- PAGE ga=26 pdf_page=72 printed_page=70 -->

Whereas, Insurance, Annuities and Relief, attempts to honor this and other
directives of the General Assembly in the investment practices of the
PCA,

Whereas, access to in-room pornographic movies is a source of severe
temptation to some of the delegates of the General Assembly as
evidenced in personal confessions;

Whereas, access to in-room pornographic movies may allow for covenant
children to be exposed without willful intent to sinful images and
temptations;

Whereas, scripture reveals the principle that tells us to respect the brethren and
to be cautious not to cause them to stumble, (I Corinthians 8:13);

Whereas, scripture tells us to “Flee sexual immorality,” (I Corinthians 6:18);

Therefore, I respectfully request the 26th General Assembly of the Presbyterian Church in America to instruct the Stated Clerk’s office and any other
agencies involved in making arrangements for future General Assembly
meetings to not seek business with hotels which provide in-room
pornographic movies, or to make all such arrangements in such a way
that the hotels are required to turn-off the pornographic movie access to
all rooms in the facility being reserved for the Presbyterian Church in America during the time of the assembly and its pre-meetings.

Thanks: TEs George Long (Convenor) and H. R. Patterson; REs John Spencer and
Dennis Baxter. (See also 26-72, p. 256.)
26-21 Procedural Ruling on Committee of Commissioners Reports
The Assembly adopted a procedural motion that throughout the Assembly
Committee of Commissioners recommendations which have the unanimous approval of
the Committee of Commissioners be automatically approved unless there is an objection
to particular recommendations.
26-22 Committee of Commissioners on Interchurch Relations
TE Thomas Vanden Heuvel, Chairman, led the Assembly in prayer and presented
the report. The Assembly paused to sing “The Church’s One Foundation” . The
Chairman yielded to TE Eric Perrin, Chairman of IRC. He introduced the following
Fraternal Delegates, who each brought greetings from their respective denominations.
TE John Carson Associate Reformed Presbyterian Church
TE Mark Harrington Orthodox Presbyterian Church
TE Chun II Cho Korean American Presbyterian Church

greetings from their respective denominations:
TE Ed Davis Evangelical Presbyterian Church
TE Jerome Julian united reformed churches of north america
TE Safwat el Bayyadi, chairman of the protestant council of Egypt, introduced
Mrs. Safwat el Bayyadi, TE Soliman Sadek, pastor in Cairo and current moderator of
synod of Nile; TE Kamal Youssef, pastor in Cairo, past moderator of the Synod, and

<a id="ga26-p72"></a>

chairman of Pastoral Affairs, Committee of Synod, TE Farouk el-Deiry, pastor in
Alexandria and director of Alexandria branch of Evangelical Theological Seminary of
Cairo; and TE Reda Adly, pastor in Cairo and radio Bible teacher; and brought
greetings.
TE Scott Lindsay of the Continuing Presbyterian Church of Australia brought
greetings.
Greetings were also brought by Yo-Sung Moon, Moderator of the Korean
Presbyterian Church, and Jae Duck Ko, Moderator of the Korean Presbyterian Church
(Reformed), TE Henry Koh translating.

#### I. Business Referred to the Committee

A. Report of the Permanent Committee [see Appendix H, p. 412]
B. Minutes of Permanent Committee Meetings of August 12, 1997,
September 23, 1997, and April 17, 1998.
C. Overtures 11 and 22.
D. Communication 1.

#### II. statement of major issues discussed

A. Status of matters relating to the Christian Reformed Church and
NAPARC
B. Resolutions on Public Education and on Pornography and Obscenity
adopted by NAE.
C. permanent committee paper on categories of ecclesiastical relationships
with other churches.
D. relations with the united reformed churches of north America.
E. Appropriate responses to Overtures 11 and 22.
F. commendation of the retiring stated Clerk.

#### III. Recommendations

1. that exception to the minutes of the permanent committee for august
12, 1997, September 23, 1997, and April 17, 1997 be taken with regard
to an alternate member of the Committee serving as Chairman and voting
(BCO 14:1:11). Referred to Committee on Constitutional Business
(For response, see 26-68, First Supplemental Report, IV, p. 232)
2. That the Minutes of the meeting of the Permanent Committee for August
12 (apart from the exception noted in Recommendation 1) be approved
with notation. Adopted
3. that the minutes of the permanent committee meeting of september 23,
1997 (apart from the exception noted in Recommendation 1) be approved
with notation. Adopted
4. That the Minutes of the Permanent Committee meeting of April 17, 1998
(apart from the exception noted in Recommendation 1) be approved with
notation. Adopted

invited to address the Assembly. Adopted

<a id="ga26-p73"></a>
